reviews.title: worst room I have ever seen
reviews.text: This was the most disgusting room I have ever stayed in and not the room I purchased either.  There was blood on the walls!

reviews.title: DO NOT BOOK HERE
reviews.text:
Worst experience ever. 
I constantly travel for work and I was unfortunate enough to check in late due to a flight delay. The girl who checked me in was friendly and gave me all information I needed. In the website it says that I am able to check out late at 3pm if I chose to inform them ahead of time, and I did. In person though, a late check out is only at 1 pm. As I got to my room , I checked to see the shower as I needed to get ready to go to a conference and the shower was completely ripped out and not functional. The toilet was also not working and there was a bow if parts on the floor of the bathroom. I called downstairs to see if anyone could give me a room change and honestly I have never met such rude staff before. The girl that answered was a different girl from check in and she just made me feel bad like it was my fault that the shower was broken. They also said they couldn't get me changed too another room. I ended up getting ready at a friend's place. Also for late check in, I already knew it was at 1pm but at noon someone came and banged on my door at noon and was very aggressive in letting me know that check out was soon. This was the weirdest experience I've ever had at any hotel and I've been traveling everywhere for work this year. Never again...

reviews.title: Run down.
reviews.text: Ghetto. No valet, doors close, don’t work. Dead hotel

reviews.title: Don't stay here
reviews.text: I showed up at 12:30 and nobody was in the hotel. I had to go stay at a friends place and they still charged me.

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.text: Was very disappointed with the staff and how they treated my wife and I. I would not suggest this hotel as it is not worth the money to stay.

reviews.title: Disaster
reviews.text: They hotel is *still* undergoing renovations but that wasn’t the issue.. the neighbors SMOKING METH next door was and the staff refused to move me to another room after they threw them out at 5AM. I left.  BE VERY AFRAID of this dump and horrible customer service.
reviews.room_type: Queen Room with Two Queen Beds

reviews.title: Did not get what I paid for
reviews.text: The room was not remodled. Alot of mild and water damage

reviews.title: Terrible.
reviews.text: I was greeted with staff that cared but management was so unhelpful and uncaring of the situation I was put in. I didn’t feel safe someone tried to open the door aggressively at 2am and they did nothing to help me. I was alone and they didn’t even put me in another room or try to compensate me in any way. I didn’t get any sleep at all because I was so scared. They didn’t care and couldn’t even fake a caring tone.

reviews.title: Worst hotel experience in my life.
reviews.text:
I was shocked. It’s a side road motel with St Regis name. Nice lobby and building outside but this is the only nice thing I can say.  Halls and rooms with damaged finishes, cracked ceiling, stains on the hall carpets. Bathroom looks like asylum torture room in horror movie. Cracked mirror, broken tv, no blackout curtains or any curtains at all so you can enjoy street lights all night long. No thermostat in the room to adjust temperature. No restaurant or even gym at the “hotel”. 
I’ve stayed in St Regis before but this… this one only has its name. I suppose to stay few nights but left after the first one. 
It’s a stain on St Regis name and total embarrassment .  St Regis Detroit should be shut down immediately.

reviews.title: Dingy, dated, uncomfortable
reviews.text: To start, the ally that leads to on- site, unsecured parking has such big potholes that I was concerned I would damage my vehicle. The gentleman at the desk was not helpful in locating a bellhop cart and we had several items that needed to go up to the room. We ended up making two trips.  Walking down the hall to our room we noticed one door looked like it had been kicked in and had painters tape covering the hole where the door handle should have been. There was drywall dust everywhere. Our room was so dingy, the grout was stained, tiles cracked, hair on the toilet. Our thermostat was turned to the coldest setting. When I tried to warm it up it started flashing red. I called the desk several times before I got an answer. When I finally spoke with someone he asked if I wanted to "just turn it off" for the rest of the stay since I said it was a comfortable temp at the moment. When I said "no", that I would like to be able to control the temp in the room later in the evening he asked, "Well what temp would you like it to be?" as if I should just be satisfied with what it was at in the moment. They ultimately sent staff promptly to address the issue, which I appreciated. Overall, the room was cramped, lighting was poor, and I felt like I had springs poking me through the mattress. Oh, and the Wi- Fi was so weak I had to turn it off! I would not recommend.

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.text:
This hotel was bad
No food at all. Under renovation????
Room a/c heating impossible to regulate 
Fan kept on stopping and starting. Could not sleep 
Hotel and room is very tired  

Should be shut down for. Major renovation

reviews.title: Deception by photos
reviews.text:
I arrived at the hotel. The parking situation was awkward ( infront) I grabbed the dirty Cary. The clerk was so well dressed and nice (shorter  lighter female) I checked in with her. It was fast and easy. My Aunt was allowed to park out front. We went up to the room 203 the lobby fooled me. 
I walked into a motel 6! Depressing as ever Damage to the wall (left side) small holes included. Poor lighting.  Old outdated lamps non adjustable.  Dirty, dirty! Tub causing ard it was so dirty and peeling up same ard the tub. 
Sat on the bed and started feeling like I was being poked with tiny pins/ biting. I called the front desk. 
Different brown girl . Nasty attitude immediately! I explained my disappointment and uncleanness I was seeing. She said well you are on the 2nd floor! Like I hand picked my room. She said our new rooms start on the 6th floor but those are our renovated newer rooms but . Idk what you want me to do a abt what you just to me!? Me: Miss the room is dirty! She said soo.... you called me for..... I  said your right no problem. I will be bringing your keys down She said good. I did so and left. It's always your own. Embarrassing a d bitter.
I moved on I WILL NEVER EVER RETURN.

reviews.title: DO NOT RECOMMEND
reviews.text: The hotel room we got was NOTHING like the pictures. Instead the hotel was clearly overbooked and they threw us on a floor that was still being renovated. Our room had many stains on the carpet, and we paid for a 2 queen bed room. WE ONLY GOT ONE QUEEN BED. And there was nothing the staff can do. Three of us had to share the bed, because everytime we would go back to the front to ask for a 2 bedroom, and even a cleaner room. They declined. Just pay a few more bucks for a room downtown.
reviews.room_type: Queen Room with Two Queen Beds

reviews.title: This was bad
reviews.text: This is a historical building which is fine but keep that in mind. The lobby and bar and beautiful. However the rooms are beyond outdated. In the first room we were assigned was on the 3rd floor and  TV had static. The bathtub drain was clogged. The toilet and shower had stains. The heater was so loud it woke me up out of my sleep when it would turn on. We asked to be moved to a new room. Which took about 40 minutes but we did get a newer cleaner room. The new room was fine but the hotel rooms are very basic. The staff was friendly but they were definitely prioritizing enjoying themselves at work in stead of actually working.
reviews.room_type: Queen Room with Two Queen Beds

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.text: Do not book this hotel.

reviews.title: Not remodeled
reviews.text: I booked based off photos and a recent remodel stated. It was horrible,  smelled old, looked old,was old.

reviews.title: WORST HOTEL EVER
reviews.text: VERY DIRTY

reviews.title: Horrible Customer service
reviews.text: Tv didn't work. Ended up in a room with double beds instead of the king that I booked.  Desk clerk was extremely rude, bathroom was filthy as hell

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.text: Charged for 2 stays!
reviews.room_type: Queen Room with Two Queen Beds

reviews.title: Just bad.
reviews.text: Website and hotel signs say valet $20 so I waited awhile and when I finally got up to the front nobody came. I went in and asked where valet was. They said they didn’t have any and if I self park on site (unclear where that even is) it’s $20.  The thermostat/heat in the room did not work at all. I called down and they said they’d send someone. I waited ten minutes and called again, at that point I had to deal with it because check out was approaching and their only solution was to move me and all my belongings to another room for the one hour left of my stay lol. They took PHOTO COPIES of my debit card because their computers weren’t working to hold the incidental charge. This place is not even downtown, nothing good about it. LOUD people next to me, like having a party. Will never stay here again. Detroit has so much better to offer

reviews.title: Terrible
reviews.text: I booked a room 2 queen beds.   I got 1 full bed no sofa on a unremodkes room.   How in the hell is 4 people to share 1 full bed.   It wasn't even a king.   My reservation shows 2 queen bed booked.   I was to there was no other rooms and  booking through 3rd party didn't gaurabtee beds.   That's not what my options were.  I chose the option to book 2 queens.   It said nothing about no guarantee.  I took picks and disputing this charge.   Hotel staff was no help said they had no more rooms.   I was flabbergasted.    It was unbelievable I walked in that room to a nightmare.
reviews.room_type: Queen Room with Two Queen Beds

reviews.title: Terrible hotel
reviews.text: I checked in at 4pm, went to the room and noticed the AC wasn’t working. The lady at the desk said she’d have someone fix it. I went out for the day and when I got back at 10pm it still didn’t work. At 1:30 am There’s loud banging on the doors up and down the hallway “utility man, Im here to turn on the AC”. He came in and opened a panel on the AC unit, it turned on for 20 minutes then it sounded like a breaker tripping and it turned off again, woke up sweating, went to take a shower and the water wouldn’t drain. Upon checking out, I mentioned the bizarre night and how disappointed I was and all she said was “I’ll let somebody know”

reviews.title: Would Not Recommend Unless You Can't Find Anything Else
reviews.text:
I mistook this hotel for the St. Regis brand I'm accustomed.

I was a bit behind the eight ball when I landed, so I ended up not changing hotels. 

Everything about this hotel was bad. 

The lady at the front desk barely looked up when sharing hotel details.

No breakfast. No ice machines on floors. The hallways were almost the same temperature as outside. (I stayed in March, it was cold.)

And when I thought it couldn't get any worse, there were unhoused people (or maybe just beggars) in the lobby - with ankle monitors to boot.

When I needed to speak with someone at night, there were signs that read, "We stepped away and will be back shortly" or something to that effect.

I try to be fair and I've never left bad reviews before, but this took the cake.

I hope they straighten up and get things together, but I would NOT recommend to anyone.

reviews.title: How the mighty have fallen
reviews.text: Ive stayed at this location several times over the years due to its convenient location near the Fisher Theatre.  The most recent time (prior to now) was the beginning of 2020 (pre-COVID).   The hotel *used* to be a little dated, but clean; with a decent restaurant and bar on site, as well as a skywalk connection over to the Fisher Theatre.  In the span of 4 years, that has completely changed.  What once used to be a lively, welcoming place to stay has obviously been neglected.  The room was legitimately falling apart; with cracks and crumbling in the plaster, a broken sink, and a sketchy shower.  The heater unit sounded like it was on its' last legs.  The restaurant is closed, the sky bridge to the Fisher Theatre is closed, the staff was polite but obviously checked out (no pun intended).  I'm afraid that I'll just have to live with the memories of what once was, as I will never be staying here again.  Which is sad, because the location is very convenient.

reviews.title: Checked out immediately
reviews.text: Water damage on the walls,  bathroom was rusted, and we could hear our neighbors entires cell phone conversations, the walls were so thin it sounded like they were in the room with us. Couldn’t sleep at all, spent half the night trying to call/find other hotels that were available, checked out 1st thing in the morning to go to another hotel.

reviews.title: Don’t do it
reviews.text: You’re better off booking a room at Motel 6 to save you some money for the same exact room. The room was dirty, curtains had holes in them and the “bed bug” cover was completely ripped to shreds. The shower head was horrendous.. barely a drizzle. The pictures of what you see are not what you will get. If you’re looking for dingy and grossness, still save money and book Motel 6.

reviews.title: No AC
reviews.text: Our room had no air conditioning.  The air blew cold for 2 or 3 minutes then got very hot.  They were going to check on it while we were out.  They put it on high and the thermostat read 99 degrees when we came back!! Our room had no smoke detector, just a where it once was.

reviews.title: Convenient
reviews.text: We needed a place close to the Fisher theater, so I chose the St Regis. Aside from being within walking distance to the theater, I really have nothing else to recommend. The hotel was relatively clean. I couldn't figure out the HVAC controls, so we just ran the fan on Hi all night. There was a puddle on the bathroom floor, coming from behind the toilet. The ice machine was inop. No vending or room service. The bar closed early. No coffee available in the morning. The clerk did refund our parking fee ($20) for our in-convenience.

reviews.title: "Hand me down hotel"
reviews.text:
1. Hotel cleaniness: 
a.hardwood floors not swept and mopped
b. toilet seat was old with actual stains and markings on it- needed a new seat
c. shower/tub had old stains in it
3.too loud as they were having a party on our floor- had to call front desk.nothin changed. (had a 13 miles to run the next morning and was terribly distrubed)
4. after running 13 miles went in to the hotel (sweaty and tired as heck!)and the front desk asked me where my mask was!!!
5. no security for cars
6. location is ideal for downtown festivities
7. price is not equivalent to value of service
8. "SAL" the front desk conceige, was the best thing for this hotel.
reviews.room_type: Queen Room with Two Queen Beds

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.text: The hotel needs to be updated.  The room has not seen any updates for quite a while.  Stains present on the chairs and carpets, curtains dirty, tub and tile stained, shower head did not work, water either too hot or too cold, room too hot and there was no way to control the heat; even when it was off, it was still heating.  The room was cleaned, though.  However, it was too far from downtown and there was nothing around to make it worthy.  Unfriendly front desk, and it was a little too expensive for what it offered.  This hotel has seen better days.  Will not stay there again and will not recommend it.

reviews.title: Nice Lobby but Below Average Rooms
reviews.text:
Received a Queen room when I paid for a King. When I checked in they initially booked me into a room that was not made, followed by checking me into a room that was the wrong bed size.   After calling in I was told that there is no option to upgrade or compensate because of the holiday.

The room itself was below expectation. The heater did not work and had to call in twice to get it repaired.  Half the lights in the bathroom were out and the tissue box was empty with just decorative pieces stuffed in it for show. there is The ceiling in the bathroom there is obvious degradation of the ceiling from the moisture and on the last day I found a small cockroach in the bathroom.

reviews.title: Terrible
reviews.text: AC too loud. NO COFFEE OR FOOD IN THE ENTIRE HOTEL.

reviews.title: Pictures inaccurate-sad motel not fancy hotel
reviews.text: Our room was not even close to the photos, nor did we get the King room we'd reserved. We got a room with 2 queen (or possibly double) beds that was equivalent to a Motel 8 room. It didn't have a fridge, there were only 2 towels, it took us 20 minutes to figure out the thermostat/heat, and it took so long for the water in the shower to get warm that we gave up waiting for it to get hot.

reviews.title: No wash clothes
reviews.text: The lobby is beautiful but the fact that they had no wash clothes and I had to pay for parking made it a not again, ever for me. Pretty sure I heard a whole fight in my hallway. Staff was polite but holy moly get it together.

reviews.title: Beware Sunday nights
reviews.text: If you want to relax on a Sunday night, DO NOT stay here. The whole building is turned into a loud nightclub every Sunday from 6pm until almost midnight. I stayed on the 3rd floor and could hear the loud music from the first floor lounge even with earplugs. If I had known that they have a DJ blasting in the bar every Sunday, I never would have stayed here. It was so loud that I couldn't hear the person at the front desk while we were both shouting. Terrible experience.

reviews.title: Looks can be deceiving
reviews.text: The room I booked looked nothing like the pics I saw. My room was very outdated. The only thing renovated in the room was the A/C unit. The shower had peeling tiles both on the wall and tub. There were used wash rags hanging from the shower rod. The towels and wash rags provided had stains on them (luckily I travel with my own. No toiletries provided in the bathroom as well (I bring my own so I didn't care). Both the iron & ironing board were broken. The hallways had dark red stains everywhere in front of the doors of different rooms. It felt more like a prison than a hotel. I went to an event that evening and was dreading going back to the room to sleep.  Definitely will not stay there again.

reviews.title: The water
reviews.text: We were checked in with no warning that the water only has hot, hotter, and hell water! My 8 year old went in for a shower and screamed when the water was so hot. Tan in to try and cool it down and nope. Called front desk and they said they have had this issue for some time and it was a hotel wide issue. Not moving rooms would help. We were told to use a wash cloth and sink bath…. No warning at time of check in or even notice. there obviously was a reason the place is cheaper then any place around down town… it’s because the water is so hot it could literally send you to the hospital from burns. The toilet also when flushed would steam up the bathroom because the water was soooo hot. Just all around no.

reviews.title: Very noisy and over priced.
reviews.text: Very noisy, and kept up with slamming doors and talking till 4:00 am.
reviews.room_type: Queen Room with Two Queen Beds

reviews.title: Disappointed
reviews.text:
It has been many, many years since I have stayed at a hotel that NO food offerings whatsoever. Also, that fact was not made clear during booking. 
There was even fresh coffee. The coffee was provided thru a machine and was awful. 
This hotel had no amenities. My biggest complaint is that without my knowledge, the parking was not free. What a scam. Do not stay here. The hotel was sketchy.

reviews.title: Outdated rooms
reviews.text: I didn’t feel safe. The toilet had flush problems.

reviews.title: Only for emergencies
reviews.text:
Can’t say I’d stay here again unless it’s an emergency last minute stay. Staff just rude and had attitude. Ice machines didn’t work and bar was not staffed. 
Hallway rug was lifted up and all stained. My door looked like the number was ripped off and the light switches didn’t all work.

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.text: very old and not updated.  the furniture was all chipped.  the TV did not work. the floors and walls all looked unclean.  looked like it all should undergo renovation.  limited in staff and had to wait a long time
reviews.room_type: Queen Room with Two Queen Beds

reviews.title: Not a good stay
reviews.text: No hot water.  Toilet barely worked and had to use lobby restroom. Heating unit did not work properly.  Stains on bedding.
reviews.room_type: Queen Room with Two Queen Beds

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.text: Room unclean. No hangers in closet. No ice machine. Staff unable to provide information on place to get food as no restaurant in hotel

reviews.title: Looks Nothing Like The Pictures
reviews.text: For the price point I paid- it definitely was not worth what I paid. The pictures on their website is no where near what the rooms like besides maybe the bar in the lobby which doesn’t seem to have anyone tending every time I passed it. A/C barely worked in room it was so hot. No room service if you ever want more toilet paper or towels which to my surprise they only had literally one roll in the room. Ice machines & vending machines don’t work on any of the floors. Location for music festivals/night clubs was good but that was about the only thing.

reviews.title: Not fully remodeled
reviews.text: Not a bad experience but the rooms are not upgraded to match the lobby, which is upgraded and sleek. Room furniture and bathroom were outdated. Again, not a terrible stay but not what I expected based on the website photos.

reviews.title: Hotel st regis review
reviews.text: Had booked a king size bed and was given a full size bed.  After speaking to front desk I was switched to a king size room.  Facility has no gym no pool no ice machine on floors no microwaves no refrigerator no shuttle no complimentary breakfast. Located 4 miles from downtown it seemed to lack many of the amenities for the price.  Being close to amtrak and q line were the only benefit.

reviews.title: At least the bartender was great!
reviews.text:
Screwed up reservation
hard worn room
staff not 'able' to correct the problems
We were AFRAID to look at the second bed in our room - a lot of weird lumps in the freshly made bed.
At least the bartender was great!  A heavy pour helps smooth out some of the issues.

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text: When we got there the couple in front of us had been sent to a room that was already occupied and they had gone in to someone else’s stuff there, our room didn’t have heat so we had to be moved and then on our second and third day there was no hot water.  We still had a lovely time in Detroit and it was really nice being next to the Qline

reviews.title: Overnight last minute visit to Detroit
reviews.text:
We decided to come & enjoy Detroit for 24 hours.
Hotel description is inaccurate: No food service. 
Clean but basics were lacking: tv did not work & when tried calling could only use the speakerphone feature… then the human who checked us in & seemed “over it” telling us we were lucky to get a king bed & said “good” when we responded we didn’t need parking, told us “do me a favor & jiggle the tv wires & it should work.” After that did not work we stopped at the front desk to let them know we’d be gone to dinner & had permission to go in to fix the tv… which didn’t happen. We called back down & the answer was “all I can do is put a service request order.” 
Room had no extra blanket, no seating, & absolutely no decor.  Light fixture on one side of the bed didn’t work. 
We will not be staying here again. It’s a beautiful building, but poorly managed.

reviews.title: Lobby is nice, rooms are bad
reviews.text: Extremely dated room, like a college dorm. Wood floors and old tile bathroom, no rugs. Everything is very old worn and dated. Shower pressure and temperature abysmal

reviews.title: Does not look like photos
reviews.text: The hotel itself is fine; however, the rooms do not look like the photos featured.  At all. If you are expecting the photos, you will be disappointed. Also, parking is $20/night on top of the room cost. Otherwise, it is a fine hotel.
reviews.room_type: Queen Room with Two Queen Beds

reviews.title: St regis
reviews.text: Exspensive no pool, no gym, nothing to do. I had to ask for a fridge  most of the staff was cool but the one guy at main desk was rude, and unhelpful

reviews.title: Missing some basics
reviews.text: Good price and  relatively conveniently located for us but missing some basics.  Had to go to the bar for ice. Room was stocked with one coffee cup and one decaf coffee pod (no regular).  Toilet seat wobbled. Took forever to get hot water in the shower. Pathetic TV service with no guide and limited channel selection. Freezer compartment of dorm frig completely frozen over.  Just overall seemed poorly maintained.
reviews.room_type: Queen Room with Two Queen Beds

reviews.title: False advertising
reviews.text:
Pictures of hotel room do not match.
Very old facilities

reviews.title: Odd
reviews.text: First, the light switch entering the room didn’t work. Next the HVAC broke. We asked about breakfast. There was none and staff had no idea where we could find some in the Neighbourhood.  We ended up at a White Castle. Plus: it was close to the train station.

reviews.title: Not a 3 star hotel
reviews.text: No pool, no food or room service available, unsecured  parking, rooms inconstant, scolding water, told facilities in bathroom, no refrigerator, large gap in door letting light in (needed to pad with towel). No cleaning room service between nights. Staff more interested in texting on their phone and then helping customers standing in front of them. No ice machine on floor had to go to bar to receive it.

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text: The three nights we stayed in this hotel we couldn’t even brush our teeth without burning our mouth.   We did not have cold water in the room.  After complaining they changed our room which made no difference.  Something is drastically off with their cold water system in the rooms.

reviews.title: No Coffee
reviews.text: No coffee in lobby... directed me to go down street.  All coffee/shops within walking closed (Monday).

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text: First room, no cold water, moved to second room, no hot water.

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text: Air was not working and room was very hot and stuffy. Walls are thin heard guest next door and across the hall.
reviews.room_type: Queen Room with Two Queen Beds

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text:
We got to tell hotel at 14:00, we were told that manager is checking rooms. 20 minutes left, we did not receive the room and went for the lunch. No apologies. 
We ordered waik up call at 6:00AM, received it at 4: 22 AM, no apologies. I was asking to repeat waik up at 6:00AM and received it at 6:10AM. No apologies.
 When we booked this hotel, I was asking for the quiet room for 7 nights, we had received the room # 222 - the noisiest place in the hotel. Next day we had to change the room.

The wending machine does not work properly. 
There is no breakfast in the hotel at all.
reviews.room_type: Queen Room with Two Queen Beds

reviews.title: Disappointed
reviews.text: I was forewarned by the existing reviews but I went ahead and booked it anyways. It’s a beautiful hotel from the outside, even into the lobby. A nice little bar…but no restaurant (no room service).  The bathroom was a decent size (found a hair in the tub that wasn’t mine). Fridge, microwave, and coffee maker was a huge plus. TV was on the small side. I couldn’t get Netflix to work so I watched re-runs of the Office.  Blackout curtains definitely played a role in how well I slept. The beds were very comfortable. I rolled out of bed in the morning to make a cup of coffee, and right as I was about to jump back in, I noticed a black speck. Lo and behold, a bug. Judging the lack of dimension, it was in the bed with me all night. If I was staying multiple nights at this hotel, I would have checked out early.
reviews.room_type: Queen Room with Two Queen Beds

reviews.title: A/C did not work, staff did nothing.
reviews.text:
Elevator was broken and malfunctioning. My room’s A/C was also broken on a very hot and humid night but staff could do nothing. They promised to “see if a staff member could help or something” but nothing came through. A very uncomfortable night. 

Rooms are barely decorated and haven’t been updated in a while. They would be serviceable as places to sleep as long as the A/C happens to be working…

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text: It was ok

reviews.title: In desperate need of a reurb
reviews.text: The room was like staying in a 2 star motel: threadbare carpets, torn wallpaper, holes in walls, corroded bathroom fittings, lumpy pillows and duvet.  Only one working lift and such badly fitted carpet in the corridors that it was a trip hazard.

reviews.title: Very basic
reviews.text: Older hotel with only the basic necessities. No valet. Comfy beds.

reviews.title: Decent location, poor service
reviews.text: A decent stay and location marred by a complete lack of service when I left some valuables at the hotel. No call as promised and two emails not replied to.
reviews.room_type: Queen Room with Two Queen Beds

reviews.title: Not worth the money
reviews.text: This must have been the cheapest and laziest hotel renovation known to man.  It was difficult to drown out the music from the ballroom downstairs from the second floor, which went on until 2am.

reviews.title: Old Hotel
reviews.text: Hotel is old and needs some work.

reviews.title: Hotel has room for improvement
reviews.text:
Got a room for one night, and I'm glad it was just a place to crash and not my base of operations for several days. My room was the most sparsely furnished hotel room I've stayed in. Kind of amateur looking paint job, not a single decoration on the walls, no desk, so the room felt very empty. The floors were dirty and needed to be swept. In the morning I saw two large bugs (NOT roaches or bed bugs, but I'm not sure what they were) crawl from the baseboard of the walls across the floor and into the bottom of the arm chair. That grossed me out big time and was disappointing. Bathroom was cramped and the shower head is placed oddly low on the wall, and I'm not particularly tall, but it was clean in there. Besides the floors being dirty, the bed and linens were clean and very comfortable, there were no complaints there at all. The air conditioning is upgraded and very easy to adjust to your comfort.

The common areas of the hotel like the lobby and hallways are nice enough and newly renovated. But the elevators needed a good sweep and there were some flies buzzing around me in the lobby when I was waiting to go out. The hotel bar wasn't open until after 5pm the night I arrived because the bartender was late. As a fellow service industry person I can understand the lack of motivation to show up for a hotel with so few guests on a Friday night. But I was like damn I kind of wanted to start the night off with a good drink, so that was a lost opportunity since the bar looked very appealing to sit at.

The morning after, I asked the front desk guy where I could get an espresso or latte and he told me about Gathering Co, in his words, just about two blocks down Grand. In reality it's like 6 or 7 blocks and 10-15 minutes away and not all of those blocks are very pleasant to pass by if you don't know the area, I was kind of stressed thinking guy where did you just send me? When you're hung over and just want your first morning coffee and you're stuck walking under the hot summe

reviews.title: Can do better
reviews.text: The hotel is being renovated.  This means that drilling and hammering start at 8 AM.  We were here for my daughter's wedding at the Fisher Bldg.  The sound insulation was terrible as we could hear the guests  talking next door.  Several other guests noted bathroom ceilings that were collapsing.  The restaurant was not open for breakfast.  We enjoyed the location.  The staff was friendly.

reviews.title: Not the cleanest hotel
reviews.text: This hotel has seen better days. The water pressure wasn't great and the room was not cleaned well. The location is not far from downtown, so that's a plus, but they really need to do a better job of updating their rooms.

reviews.title: Disappointing
reviews.text:
Very disappointing,   Booked due to a family member having to have a significant procedure at Henry Ford Hospital.  The location is very convenient; however after check I. The entire hotel lost power for several hrs with no updates by staff.  No weather emergency or natural causes.    We were on the 6th floor and when I went down the stairs to see what was going on 1/2 the emergency lights were not working.  Very small inadequate flashlights were position at several of the floors on a chair propping open the floor door so that you could see.   It was after dark when all this happened and certainly hazardous issue.  

Than the next morning,  we woke up and had zero hot water.  When the front desk was contacted we were only told that “numerous” rooms have reported the same issue “and that it must be a building issue and the maintenance was on the way into work.”   This was 0730-8 am time frame.  

No apologies by staff or offers to mediate the disappointment in the facility

reviews.title: I didn't think it was open.
reviews.text: We booked in the evening and drive over to Detroit, arriving around 11:30 pm. There were zero lights on, we drove past the building 3 times thinking we must be at the wrong place. Finally, we just parked and decided to try. There was a person behind the counter, in the dark, shining a flashlight in our faces. Didn't really greet us, just said "The generator is out, if you had gotten here 20 minutes ago, it would have been on." Um, what? We sat in the apartment side lounge for 20 minutes, then we're told power was back, but we couldn't check in because the computers weren't working. We were given keys and shown the steps because there was no way I was getting in the elevator in a brown out, and told they'd call us when we could check in, and we'd have to come back down. We got upstairs, the room was in kind of rough shape. We needed it for 12 hours and to sleep, otherwise it would have been a bigger deal. But, refresh the chipped paint.... Finally they call to tell me that I can come down to check in. I'm tired at this point, and I can't get ready for bed because I'm still waiting. I go downstairs and the computer is still not ready, stand there for another 10 minutes. We are pushing 1am by the time I get back to the room. Get in the bathroom to brush my teeth, and there's another power failure. We go to bed and every time the power goes out, the hear goes off. We wake up 5 times to deal with it. The last time, we don't get up, and when we get up in the morning, it is 50° and we are cold,vyired and grumpy. We take none of this out on the employees and leave politely. But we should have received a large discount for the awful accommodations.

reviews.title: Great location, but a bit run down
reviews.text:
I will start by saying the location is great. You are one 15 minute bus ride from downtown Detroit but you are not paying downtown prices. A bus fare is $2, and will last you 4 hours. Because you are only one bus ride from downtown Detroit, you can get to and from the airport without an Uber that'll cost you an arm and a leg. 

The hotel itself is ok. I had a room with 2 double-sized beds. The room was showing signs of age and could definitely use some work. The air conditioner was not working so the room's air was stale. I did manage to open the window but there are many vehicles going by so it may be noisy to any light sleepers. Overall, it did the job. There was no convenience store nearby, and the hotel itself does not have a little shop to buy any snacks or drinks. The vending machine was not working when I was there so I had to use DoorDash to order bottles of water.  Make sure you stock up before going back to your room!
reviews.room_type: Queen Room with Two Queen Beds

reviews.title: Mediocre
reviews.text: If you’re looking to stay away from downtown but close enough to uber or drive, then this works. But elevators were hot, room was clean but could use some decoration and couch chairs. Their coffee from the coffee machine was great though.

reviews.title: Good for the price
reviews.text: Ok hotel. Dated and some maintenance issue. For example, room door was not closing properly so you have to slam it for it close properly. Drain plugs are dirty, shown in other pics. Area around is ok but have to be careful. Bar downstairs is not a real bar.

reviews.title: Just Okay.
reviews.text:
Lots of street level noise. 
There were plaster cracks in ceiling. The heating was very noisy and not adjustable to turn fan down to low. The TV reception was very grainy/poor.  The door would stick - had to really push/pull hard to open.  Some rooms did have refrigerators, ours did not.

reviews.title: Good value, a bit misleading
reviews.text: The lobby is gorgeous, but the rooms leave a lot to be be desired. We didn’t have toilet paper, and the room didn’t with many of the essentials! A good place to hang your hat and nothing more.

reviews.title: Ehhh
reviews.text:
The hotel is nice and clean with minor cosmetic issues in the rooms. Hotel staff were friendly. I did have to pay for parking now this is my issues with my stay…  I did not even get to use the small parking lot and still had to pay for parking lot due to an event that went until 2:30am with loud music that I could hear and fell the bass in my room in the 3rd floor…  The “lounge” was completely packed full of people and my significant other and I could barely get to the elevator to get to our room. a man made us show our key card to even walk to the Elevators because of the event and then of course there were people blocking the elevator giving us rude looks. We ordered take out and the service said they could bring it up to our room… they denied him access because of this event. 
I wish they would have told us prior that this event was going on and how late it was going to be. 
I love the style and old times feel but I don’t think I would stay here again.

reviews.title: No warnings about issues
reviews.text: Although the hotel itself looks nice in the lobbies, the room was a bit bare bones and lacked charm. Additionally, there were no notices that the water system through the entire building has a problem of only

reviews.title: Okay, but…
reviews.text: The staff was pretty much non existent, the bar wasn’t open and I had to use vending machines to get a bottle of water…  requested a late check out which wasn’t given either, never saw a smiling employee which just makes the whole vibe off. The cleanliness was decent and the bed was very comfortable. They do not have good tv service, if I didn’t get a good deal on this room through Agoda it wouldn’t have been worth the full price they list it at.

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text: Hotel was under construction so the bar area was closed.  Our room had two queen beds but only 2 sets of towels.  There was no tissue box in the room or spare toilet paper roll which I thought was odd.  Parking was easy to get to and staff was very nice.
reviews.room_type: Queen Room with Two Queen Beds

reviews.title: Rip off
reviews.text: All the amenities are closed it’s not posted anywhere but charge me $20 extra to park my vehicle overnight.
reviews.room_type: Queen Room with Two Queen Beds

reviews.title: Old and broke down...
reviews.text: The room was very expensive and small. The water in the shower would not get hot and the room stayed cold. Parking was $20 per day for guests, but only $10 for visitors. Front desk staff was indifferent. Two beds but only one package of caffeinated coffee. An old hotel charging a lot of money.
reviews.room_type: Queen Room with Two Queen Beds

reviews.title: Not really worth it
reviews.text: Paid about *** for the room, Had to pay to park my own car in their lot.. No handsoap in the bathroom, old blanket(was lumpy and shouldve clearly been thrown away. The pillows were awful. I also had no hot water for my shower the next morning. I tried for a long time but the water never got past kinda luke warm. The top of my head was hotter than the water. Overall not a great experience. The front desk service when we arrived were professional and pleasant.

reviews.title: Stay
reviews.text: Clean, nice lobby area, safe area. Room needed some furniture updating and touch up painting
reviews.room_type: Queen Room with Two Queen Beds

reviews.text: Decent place to stay.  Our HVAC wasn’t working the best but the room was nice.  There was construction going on outside the hotel at the beginning of every morning.  The road noise seemed to be quite considerable.   Overall it was a quick stay.  Overnight parking was an additional $20 a day.

reviews.title: Only plus was the location & staff
reviews.text: We chose this spot to stay because it was convenient to the spot where we were hosting a work event. Unfortunately it’s a terrible hotel. 6 of us stays here and most of us had broken air conditioning that would only stay on for 5-10 min at a time. My room was 80+ degrees most of my stay. The staff was helpful in sending someone up to look at the AC units, but they only had temporary fixes. We were offered other rooms, but if 6 AC unit are broken in 6 different rooms, would changing rooms actually help? Great location for where we needed to be, but I would definitely not stay here again.

reviews.title: Fisher Theater
reviews.text: Stayed here for a night out at the Fisher Theater. The cost was reasonable the staff were great but the room was very minimalist or thrown together I can’t decide.  There was not much to do in the hotel after the theater other then the hotel bar that not the most inviting, or appealing.

reviews.title: Disappointing
reviews.text: I hoped for more from this place, which is so close to some amazing Detroit landmarks, but perhaps since there's no other hotel options close by, there hasn't been motivation to make it excellent. The room was spartan, pergo floor instead of carpeting, outdated bed and desk chair and lack of bathroom tissue supply. Not good enough for the price.

reviews.title: Pretty basic
reviews.text: Comfy bed, beautiful lobby. Very close to the Q line. That’s the three stars. Our room looked like some furniture had been removed so there was a very large open space. The only other seating was a small wooden chair. Bathroom not renovated, toilet barely flushed even when holding the handle down.  Water pressure in shower was medium and water was not super warm. Shade broken so sleeping in was not an option, curtains on the sides would have also helped block out some light. Pretty far away from all the things, sports venues, food etc but a 30ish minute free Q line ride gets you there.

reviews.title: Not the best
reviews.text: The first room they checked me into had a large dead bug between the window and the bed on the floor. They gave the option to switch rooms or have someone come up to remove the bug. I switched rooms. The second room had no issues other than the fact that it and the prevous one were not updated as shown in pictures. Staff was very nice though.

reviews.title: Rooms
reviews.text: Rooms were nice but a little on the rundown side. Missing towel hooks in bathroom, access panel open in shower. You have to request room to be cleaned by house keeping versus it being done daily and lastly parking is not included you're billed at the end of your stay...

reviews.title: Do your research
reviews.text: The website looked lovely but I wish I read the reviews a bit more, as I was pretty disappointed. The lobby and the hotel in general are beautiful but the rooms were like staying in a warehouse. They were completely empty, no wall decor, locks on the door were questionable and had an incident with cleanliness when we checked in.  The staff was amazing and accommodating and gave us an upgrade but in general was disappointed in the quality of the upgrade.

reviews.title: Not worth it
reviews.text:
In my honest opinion! It’s not worth the amount paid to stay here! My room heat just stopped working outta nowhere! Front desk staff was nice to change my room to a warmer unit once contacted as maintenance was already gone for the day! The atmosphere is great, nice bar in lobby friendly front desk staff but the rooms need updates and working heat! No way the heat shouldn’t work in the rooms! 
But overall I enjoyed my New Years Wouldn’t go back unfortunately

reviews.title: Not worth the cost
reviews.text: Decentralized automatic AC activating multiple times in the middle of the night was so loud and exploding-like noisy that it kept waking me up without let me have a well-deserved rest after a very long car drive from Tennessee. There was no way to control the AC manually in the room. Also, reception guy told me parking would cost $10/night, but then I have been charged $20 instead for just one night. Experience not to repeat again. There are much better hotels around the area for the same price or cheaper.

reviews.title: Good for one night to see a game.
reviews.text: Not a family area, not much to see. Good for one night to see a game.

reviews.title: Good value but needs updating
reviews.text:
Considering the prices of Detroit hotels recently, the St. Regis is a pretty decent choice to stay around mid-town. 

Our biggest gripe was that our room was severely out-dated and looked nothing like the pictures we used to book. It was during the NFL Draft weekend so there was obviously no capacity to switch.

The staff were all very nice and helpful though and there are lots of great places around the hotel to walk to.
reviews.room_type: Queen Room with Two Queen Beds

reviews.title: Lions weekend
reviews.text: Great stay….little far away from downtown

reviews.title: Needs Renavations
reviews.text: When we went to check-in the person at thr front desk was more interested in flirting with their coworker than helping me. The hall carpet was dirty and stained. The room itself had a very loud a/c unit making conversation difficult, there was trash left in the trash can, the bathroom had pubic hair stuck to the ceiling.  The staff in the morning was very nice and helpful.

reviews.title: Accomodations
reviews.text: The room upgrades were very appreciated. But, the closets were removed leaving no place to hang clothes or coats. Washing of the Duvets were also appreciated as well. But, the lumps that the washing caused looks very concerning.
reviews.room_type: Queen Room with Two Queen Beds

reviews.title: Decent for the price
reviews.text: Hotel was better than I was expecting.  Decent room for the price.

reviews.title: Did the job for one night
reviews.text:
Myself and two other friends stayed here so we could attend a concert a few blocks away. We decided on this hotel because of the location to the venue, and the price seemed reasonable. 

The room was not the cleanliest of places.  There was hair in the sink, used soap left in the shower, and no extra toilet paper and the roll was almost gone.  Also the shower barely functioned. Most of the water continued to pour out of the spout and hardly any water actually came out of the shower head.  Other than the bathroom the rest of the room seemed fairly clean.
reviews.room_type: Queen Room with Two Queen Beds

reviews.title: Great staff an okay stay.
reviews.text: Location is okay. A little lack luster on amenities. The room was clean but mirrors and such were dirty with fingerprints and smears upon check in. The staff made the stay worth it. Front desk staff and bar tender were great! It’s worth a stay for the price for sure.

reviews.title: Room looked nothing like the photos
reviews.text: Our room did not match the photos. It was no where near as nice as what was advertised. Felt like a motel room.

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.text: It was a decent hotel for the money. Nothing crazy but did the job

reviews.title: Good Value
reviews.text: Close to the Fisher Theater and public transportation, but walkable restaurants options are limited. The bathroom was clean, but not the nicest shower. I would stay again at the discounted rate.

reviews.title: Decent for the price
reviews.text: Decent for the price.
reviews.room_type: Queen Room with Two Queen Beds

reviews.title: great place, unusable shower
reviews.text: The staff was wonderful. But, we were in room 606 and the shower was not functional. There is no door to enclose the water, and the showerhead literally shoots horizontally... it was absolutle unusable.
reviews.room_type: Queen Room with Two Queen Beds

reviews.title: Decent for the price
reviews.text: Hotel was clean, good location in downtown Detroit.  The room we got had a broken HVAC unit so there was no heat.  Less than ideal and we checked in later at night so they couldn't move us.  Other than that, room was clean and quiet.

reviews.title: Stay 6th time
reviews.text: If they had more amenities like food, snacks and drinks.

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.text: First room had half an inch of standing water on the entire bathroom floor. 2nd room's heating unit wouldn't turn on.  Had a 3rd room finally that was ok but very tired looking.  No cold water, I kid you not...cole water; so, a bath couldn't be taken or drinking water gotten.

reviews.title: Clean but expensive
reviews.text: Room was very clean but very basic. There is no restaurant and no breakfast offered whatsoever. My room did not have black out curtains.

reviews.title: Not bad
reviews.text: Good area

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.text: I like when they include laundry service for free for us, they got all you need.

reviews.title: Beautiful Lobby & Lounge, Standard Room
reviews.text: We had high hopes for the King room we booked, based on the gorgeous lobby & bar/restaurant that we've visited on several occasions. The room itself was no more luxurious than your basic Hilton/Hampton/etc. For the price, we were hoping for something a bit more stylish, but the rooms definitely did not get the same treatment as the rest of the hotel. The shower was actually terrible- the controls were right underneath the shower head with no way to turn it on without getting drenched. There was also no proper door, so the bathroom floor ended up all wet, and the bathmat soggy and gross. Overall, a little disappointing for the price, but for the location, I guess it's to be expected.

reviews.title: Nice hotel, but thin walls
reviews.text:
Friendly staff. Easy parking. 

But the rooms have such thin walls that you can hear people talking even from the elevator!
Also, need another mirror in the room besides the bathroom mirror.

Otherwise, a nice stay.

reviews.title: Great staff and lobby
reviews.text: The staff was friendly and helpful, the lobby had a beautiful and great ambiance. However, the rooms were very underwhelming, furniture is extremely outdated felt like walking into a motel back in the days. The room was indeed very clean but outdated. Overall great experience!

reviews.title: Nice hotel
reviews.text: Nice hotel overall. Bed was very comfortable. Two bad things were broken table in our room and no cold water.

reviews.title: Great location traveling by train but a couple issues.
reviews.text:
I came into town on the Amtrak and this location was just a block and a half away…Perfect. The Q-Line local rail line to get to Comerica Park, the DIA, and riverfront was only a couple hundred feet from the front door…Perfect. The Motown museum was only a 15-minute walk down Grand Avenue…Perfect. Security ensured I had a room key before letting me into the hotel when I returned from the baseball game…Perfect. 

I had two issues: 1. The air conditioner could not keep up. And the outside temperature was not bad. It was running constantly and super-loud but it couldn’t keep up. And 2. The temperature control for the shower was impossible. There was no warm or cold, it went straight to hot or scalding. 

I attributed my problems with a possible “we give prepay customers the rooms with issues” excuse…..But who knows.

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.text: Hotel was fine, room was clean, but te shower was entirely hot water, worst shower I have ever seen or had.

reviews.title: Not bad for a night
reviews.text: If you're only staying a night or 2 it's not a bad hotel. I've stayed here twice so far but only for 1 night. It's pretty on the outside but once you get up to the rooms it's another story. The walls are chipped, carpets are stained, our toilet kept running. This place definitely needs an update! We also used the stairs instead of the elevator cause they were all full at check out and the staircase was in shambles. Looked like it was part of a rage room, there was pictures on the ground, parts of the walls all over the floor, dirty and gross. But like I said, if you need it for just the evening so you don't drive home under the influence it will do.

reviews.title: Clean but old
reviews.text: There were mini fridges or no closet space. Tile floors and the hotel showed its age. Bed was comfortable.

reviews.title: It was just a place to rest my head
reviews.text: We stayed for one night, it was all just ok. The staff was great and it was cleanish, the hallway to our room had red stains and someone left their pizza boxes outside their door and they were never picked up. The room also had a lot of flaws, the drywall behind the bathroom door was broken, the door to the adjoining room didn't have a door handle, and the room was so cold it was like stepping into a deep freezer. I tried so hard to figure out how to change the temperature but the system on the unit did not work properly.  We had guest services come and help and it helped only increase the temp 4 degrees and then the system failed. We were both freezing! All in all for one night we survived but I wouldn't extend my stay, I'd find somewhere else, spend the extra money on quality.

reviews.title: Exceptional hospitality in the midst of renovations
reviews.text: This hotel is undergoing extensive renovations, and some areas definitely feel like construction zones. With that said, I felt the price reflected the current situation and the outstanding friendly service by the staff more than made up for the "dust" and the lack of certain amenities.

reviews.title: Location
reviews.text: My son and I spent one night at this hotel strictly due to my mother being on life support at Henry Ford hospital and this hotel was nearest to hospital.
reviews.room_type: Queen Room with Two Queen Beds

reviews.title: Comfort, class, history
reviews.text: We came in for our young daughter to walk in a fashion show and decided to stay the night as Detroit is my wife's favorite city. The view from our room looked right at the Cadillac Place and the hotel is a brief walk from the Q and bus line. It is a bit noisy due to being in a busy street, but that is absolutely to be expected. Coffee machine in the lobby was down. Forced us to explore the area a bit more to get a good cup of coffee. Found Joe Louis Southern Kitchen just around the corner. A must! We will be back to stay for sure!

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.text: There was a fire alarm that went off on our first night stay and we were accommodated.  Thank you.
reviews.room_type: Queen Room with Two Queen Beds

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.text: I was not happy with having to pay $20.00 per day to park.  Other than that it is a nice, clean place to stay.

reviews.title: Not bad
reviews.text: Good area!

reviews.title: Not bad, not amazing
reviews.text: Hotel was under some construction and there were not a lot of amenities.  Room was fine but we couldn't get the air conditioning to work and no one ever came to fix it after we complained.
reviews.room_type: Queen Room with Two Queen Beds

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.text: The hotel is a little bit worn at the edges, but it's got everything you need & the staff were very helpful. Very convenient to the tram line & the Amtrak station. Having the microwave tucked around the corner so the light doesn't keep you awake was a nice touch.
reviews.room_type: Queen Room with Two Queen Beds

reviews.title: Good Experience
reviews.text: Check in was fast. When we entered our room, an Ozone machine was on a table. When we called the reception desk, we were told that the previous guest had smoked in the room and we were given the wrong room. We were quickly given another room. The rest of the stay was uneventful. The staff we interacted with were professional and friendly.

reviews.title: Over Night for the Game
reviews.text:
Booked a 1 night stay yo go to a Pistons Raptors basketball game. Location could be better. Right at the Q Train so easy to get to the stadium. Staff very friendly and knowledgeable offered some great suggestions for lunch as was 1st time in area. Room clean and beds comfort. Parking onsite for a very reasonable price. After the game we grabbed some Happy Pizza from right behind the hotel thanks for making a great night for the kids 
Booked on the website for a great price.
reviews.room_type: Queen Room with Two Queen Beds

reviews.title: Concert at Little Caesar’s Arena
reviews.text: The QLine is 1 minute from the hotel.  Went downtown and had a pizza before the concert and then back to the hotel - free.  The hotel room didn’t have a microwave, the bar hours are sketchy, and we didn’t even ask about food.  The hotel serves its purpose and was relatively inexpensive.  Kenya at the front desk was super friendly and knowledgeable.  Good wifi.
reviews.room_type: Queen Room with Two Queen Beds

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.text: Nice. But the internet showed having a pool and spa. It does not.  And it’s expensive to park, esp in an uncovered lot.
reviews.room_type: Queen Room with Two Queen Beds

reviews.title: Business Trip
reviews.text: Staff Very Helpful and knowledgable. Room and sheets quite clean and neat. Large room too.  Located on Qline lets you get to downtown easily if so desired. Affordable bar too open in evenings! 3RD TIME  staying here, never had any issues, always clean and pleasant.

reviews.title: Hotel St. Regius
reviews.text: Really enjoyed our stay. Staff was extremely helpful. Very safe very friendly. We will for sure be returning. The lobby was absolutely beautiful. The rooms were eh but the view was amazing so no complaints especially for the price.

reviews.title: Cleanliness
reviews.text: The room was very clean, staff was helpful and very nice.  However, our room was missing furniture and light s didn't work next to beds but we were compensated.   Great location if going to the Fisher or hop on the Qline a 2 minute walk to head downtown, all sport complexes or Campus Martius!

reviews.title: Friendly staff and central location
reviews.text:
The staff made my stay memorable.  It would be a disservice to highlight an individual since they were equally friendly and helpful, providing exceptional service.

The location is worth noting since it provides easy (and free via Q line) access to downtown and other cultural attractions.  The Fisher Building is a block away and there’s a terrific Philippino bakery around the corner.

Some comments about rooms are fair, but the issues should be rectified once the renovations are complete.

reviews.title: Comfortable stay in Detroit
reviews.text:
Was positioned perfectly for access to Henry Ford Hospital 
Very close to the Q line for free access to downtown 
Quite good food options within a very short walk of the hotel
Comfortable clean rooms
No pool,gym or food options in the hotel which was not an issue for us but might be for some

reviews.title: Great Revived Landmark
reviews.text: Maybe not as luxurious as its heyday, but still a historic building with incredible views of downtown Detroit and plenty of spots worth a visit within walking distance. Very attentive and knowledgeable staff!

reviews.title: Comfortable and easy
reviews.text: A great location for a weekend trip to see the city

reviews.title: Concert
reviews.text: Our stay was good we came for one night for the AC/DC concert. There was a happy hour drink special before the concert. The bartender was awesome. No complaints. I’d go back no problem.

reviews.title: Good deal
reviews.text: The hotel itself has beautiful decor and an amazing bar. We do wish it had a restaurant, room service would have been used for sure. The room was just basic, but that's all we needed.

reviews.title: Great location!
reviews.text: A bit disappointed that when you book with a third party you do not get the remodeled rooms. Check in was easy and staff was pleasant. Lobby was great when we entered but when we got to our floor where our room was, it looked like haunted old building. The floor was dark and actually a bit scary. Room was clean but old and dark! Bartender in hotel bar was amazing and drinks tasty. Right next to public transportation. Would definitely stay again but in an updated room.
reviews.room_type: Queen Room with Two Queen Beds

reviews.title: Happy with stay
reviews.text: Nice location near downtown. Not fancy but a nice clan room at a nice rate. There is a $20 parking fee.

reviews.title: Hotel is nice, under renovation
reviews.text: If you get one of the renovated rooms, it is nice. If not - it does feel quite outdated. However they are working on it.
reviews.room_type: Queen Room with Two Queen Beds

reviews.title: Movement
reviews.text: Great place to stay parking was a little expensive but overall fantastic experience
reviews.room_type: Queen Room with Two Queen Beds

reviews.title: Aging but still great
reviews.text: I ended up in a renovated room with a city view. It was great. Spacious, bathroom was in good shape (some wall and ceiling issues) but overall, it was a great value. Close to the QLine factors into the assessment.

reviews.title: Nice place
reviews.text: We enjoyed our stay at St Regis. Very clean, spacious rooms. If you're there on a Sunday they even have music/dancing with cocktails at the bar on the main floor. Only complaint is they don't have an ice machine, so you have to get ice from the bar and no refrigerators in the rooms.

reviews.title: Prefect get away right in down town
reviews.text: Excellent

reviews.title: Good place for value
reviews.text: On weekend, there is nothing much nearby and not much walkable but hotel and facilities are good.

reviews.title: Excellent
reviews.text: Very friendly place.

reviews.title: Good Experience
reviews.text: Rooms were decent and clean. Cleaning in the lobby and elevators could have used a little more attention but not terrible. Desk staff very professional and friendly. Biggest miss is no breakfast option at hotel. Even prepackaged items for purchase would be appreciated.Depending on amount you pay, well worth it.

reviews.title: Pleasant stay
reviews.text: I came for a concert in downtown Detroit. The hotel was well kept, easy to get to, and at a fair price. I enjoyed my stay and recommend this place.

reviews.title: Premium king room was great!
reviews.text: After reading several negative reviews after booking, I was nervous but very happy with our premium king room! The view was great and being on the same block as the Fisher Theater, the reason for our visit, was so convenient!! The hotel room was a little basic, as in the floor and furniture were all wood and no rug or artwork so it was a bit echo-y, but the bed was comfy and room was very clean, which are the important parts! Plus the lamps by the bed were a really nice soft white, rather than the horrible harsh bright white lighting that so many hotels choose, so it felt cozy! Would definitely book again :-)

reviews.title: Perfectly Decent Place.  Very Convenient
reviews.text:
We needed to be at Henry Ford early in the morning with a snowstorm expected so we stayed here. 
Good:  Very close to Henry Ford.
The room was clean, the bed was pretty comfortable, the water was hot, check in was cordial and easy.   The fridge and coffee maker were in good shape and doing their job.  It was a spacious room.  It had a newer heater/AC unit.  Parking was easy.  We got a good price for that Thursday, less than $100.
The Meh:    It is an older remodel so you will see wear here and there.  In our room the chain on the blind was disconnected and wouldn't go up.   I really would appreciate a small microwave which it did not have.  The new heater had a loud and heavy blowing fan you could not turn off completely.

reviews.title: Excellent

reviews.title: Great Location and Lobby, but Some Guest Rooms Need Updating
reviews.text:
The St. Regis has a great location, especially if you're planning to visit the Fisher Theater, which is just a short walk away. The lobby is beautiful and welcoming, giving a lovely first impression.

However, our room felt somewhat outdated, which was a bit disappointing considering the elegance of the common areas. With a few modern updates, the rooms could match the appeal of the lobby. It would also be fantastic if the restaurant was open, as that would make dining options more convenient.

Overall, a decent stay, but it could be even better with a few improvements.

reviews.title: Charming classic hotel
reviews.text:
I loved the feel of a hotel designed in the glory days of Detroit and renovated for modern day travelers.  The restaurant served breakfast, lunch and dinner👏🏻 We had the adult macaroni n cheese on two consecutive nights.  Packed with big chunks of lobster and brimming with a cheesy/buttery sauce, it was superb.  The wait staff was attentive and friendly.
Staff was helpful and when we had an issue with our bathroom, housekeeping showed up within 5 minutes to bring fresh towels and supplies.
This hotel is a short walk to the Fisher Theater which can be accessed by a secure walkway off the dining room or by a short walk.  We chose the street and felt perfectly safe.
It was a wonderful stay.  Bravo

reviews.title: Stayed during remodel and still nice
reviews.text: This is an old Detroit hotel that is very quaint and has a lot of character. They are in the middle of a remodel and are missing some soft touches like wall pictures and rugs but the room was large with a comfortable bed and clean, soft bedding.  I was there on a Thursday so it was very quiet. Loads of restaurants to choose from just a few minutes away. Great location and ample on-site parking.

reviews.title: Good choice

We didn't get a ton of time to explore all of the amenities because we went to a Lions game. The hotel was an easy ride on the highway to the game & everything in central Detroit. Thanks for a great stay Hotel St. Regis!

reviews.title: What An Amazing Venue
reviews.text:
Was in town for a wedding but wanted to check out Hotel St. Regis for the name alone because I'm an actor and director who is shooting  a short film that has St. Regis (a made up school not hotel) in the script. 
Well this hotel is has great architecture, friendly staff and I wanted to lounge around on every designed seat.
In the evening there's a circular bar that comes to life. It was cool seeing the bartender setting up.
I walked around the area and found a small shopping mall, concert venue and a lot of residential streets. A few restaurants (mainly fast food) but about 7 blocks away there was a coop grocery store and a mom & pops sandwhich shop across from a gas station and liquor store.
All in all...this place is amazing.

reviews.title: Exceeded my expectations!
reviews.text: This Hotel St. Regis I must say, "Just exceeded my expectations!"  As soon as I walked in through the big glass doors my eyes made contact with the beautiful greenery of live tall fig plants, and a lobby that gave you a feeling of a French Chateau getaway. I was then greeted by Kenya who had the  most welcoming smile at the front desk and assisted me with my seamless check in. When I went into the elevator it was clean and bright, I knew I was going to be safe and secured, as there are cameras  all around. As I walked onto my floor, the aroma was amazing as the scent was like fresh, crisp and clean laundry. My room was awesome, the bed was huge and comfortable, and the decor  reminded me of a French Bistro! They provided Bath and Body Works Stress Relief Soap, and lotion, an additional touch to assist you with de-stressing from a long day conference on your feet. I also had the pleasure to interact with another one of the front desk clerk Destiny, who was just as inviting with her warm smiles and polite disposition, and took time to assist me with all the information about getting around in Detroit, Michigan! With this being my first visit to Michigan and the Hotel St. Regis, and with such upscale ambiance and royal treatment I will certainly be returning, as it was a home away from my home, and I suggest you should check it out as well!

reviews.title: Great Stay at a Great Price
reviews.text: Honestly, when deciding to come to Detroit for concert my wife and I were little nervous because of all the stories we've heard about the city. This hotel helped debunk all those fears. It was in a great spot close to little caesars arena, which was great. The lobby, bar, and grand entrance way were truly something out of a movie, the design and architecture were breathtaking.  The room was large, with a super comfy king size bed, and the room had blackout curtains which made sleeping in an easy feat. If we happen to find ourselves in Detroit again, no question, we know where will be staying for sure. Great place.

reviews.title: Ghost Hotel
reviews.text:
The front desk staff are wonderful .  The rest of the hotel feels like something out of a Scooby Doo episode.

The bar's closed. No one sits in the lobby.  Some nice French Impressionist paintings hang on the walls but they're not enough to lift the gloom.. As you wait for the middle aged man in a gorilla suit to appear, tear off his mask and say, "l'd have got away with it if it wasn't for you meddling kids..."
